content:
🔥 abtop | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Monitoring and managing the performance and resource utilization of multiple AI coding agent sessions in a terminal environment.
✨ Key Features: • Real-time monitoring of AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex CLI, OpenCode) • Tracks token usage, context window percentage, and rate limits • Detects open ports and child processes spawned by agents • Supports multiple active agent sessions across macOS, Linux, and Windows
📖 Summary: abtop is a terminal-based monitoring tool for AI coding agents, inspired by htop. It provides real-time insights into the performance and resource usage of agents like Claude Code, Codex CLI, and OpenCode, displaying token consumption, rate limits, context window status, and active network ports.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 docker-android | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: Running Android emulators in a Docker container for automated testing and CI/CD pipelines.
✨ Key Features: • Minimal Alpine based image with Android emulator and KVM support • Bundles Java Runtime Environment 11 • Customizable Android version, device type, and image types • Port-forwarding of emulator and ADB • Emulator images are wiped on restart
📖 Summary: This repository provides a minimal and customizable Docker image for running the Android emulator as a service. It's designed for headless execution, making it ideal for CI/CD pipelines and automated testing environments.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 MiroShark | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Simulating and forecasting outcomes of various scenarios using swarm intelligence agents.
✨ Key Features: • Simulate anything from press releases to policy drafts • Hundreds of grounded AI agents (Twitter, Reddit, prediction market) • Hour-by-hour simulation progression • Interactive simulation: chat with agents, drop breaking news, fork timelines • Generates reports with cited posts and trades
📖 Summary: MiroShark is a universal swarm intelligence engine that simulates complex scenarios by spawning hundreds of AI agents. These agents interact within a simulated environment, posting, arguing, and trading based on an initial input, providing insights and reports on the simulated outcomes.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 devFind | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To serve as a discoverable directory for developers to showcase their skills and find collaborators or employment opportunities.
✨ Key Features: • Search functionality for developers based on skills, location, or name • Platform for developers to showcase skills and connect with collaborators • User-friendly and searchable format for discovering developers • Developer profiles created in JSON format
📖 Summary: devFind is an open-source platform designed for developers to create profiles showcasing their skills and connect with potential collaborators. Profiles are created in JSON format and are discoverable through a user-friendly, searchable web interface.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ react-datepicker | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide developers with a flexible and easy-to-use date and time selection component within React applications.
✨ Key Features: • Reusable Datepicker component for React • Supports date and time selection • Localization support • Custom header configuration • Integration with date-fns and moment.js
📖 Summary: React Date Picker is a versatile and reusable date selection component for React applications. It offers extensive customization options, including time selection, localization, and custom header support, making it easy to integrate date and time picking functionality into your UI.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
